prompt:
Before answering, invoke /meta:socratic and work through the protocol.
Consider what type of thinking this problem actually requires before jumping
to a solution.


Before solving this problem, analyze it:
(a) What type of problem is this?
(b) What cognitive strategy is most relevant?
(c) What will the hardest part be?
Then solve it.

Respond ONLY with JSON:
{"problem_type": "...", "strategy": "...", "hardest_part": "...", "solution": "..."}

Problem: A hospital needs to schedule 4 surgeons across 3 operating rooms over
5 days. Each surgeon can perform at most 2 surgeries per day. There are
15 scheduled surgeries with the following constraints: Surgeries S1-S5
require Surgeon A or B (specialized). Surgeries S6-S10 can be done by
any surgeon. Surgeries S11-S15 require Surgeon C or D. Surgery S3 and
S7 need the same operating room (shared specialized equipment) but cannot
happen on the same day. Surgery S12 must happen before S14 (staged
procedure). No operating room can host more than 2 surgeries per day.
Provide a valid schedule or prove it is impossible.

parsed.problem_type: Constraint satisfaction / combinatorial scheduling — deterministic, bounded, verifiable by exhaustive checking
parsed.strategy: Capacity feasibility check first (rule out impossibility cheaply), then greedy assignment respecting hard constraints, verify every constraint explicitly before declaring success — resist anchoring on the first partial schedule that looks plausible
parsed.hardest_part: The S3/S7 same-OR-different-day constraint interacts with the S12<S14 ordering constraint and per-OR daily capacity; a naive greedy pass can satisfy them individually but violate them jointly — the crux is placing S7 and S3 in the same OR on different days without overloading that OR

solution.feasibility: Feasible — 3 ORs × 2/day × 5 days = 30 slots; 4 surgeons × 2/day × 5 days = 40 surgeon-slots; both exceed the 15 required surgeries with room to spare

constraint_verification.surgeon_daily_max_2: A: Day1=2,Day2=2,Day3=1 ✓ | B: Day1=2,Day2=2,Day3=1 ✓ | C: Day1=2,Day2=1 ✓ | D: Day2=1,Day3=1 ✓
constraint_verification.OR_daily_max_2: All ORs across all days have ≤2 surgeries ✓
constraint_verification.S1_to_S5_specialization: All assigned to A (qualifies as A or B) ✓
constraint_verification.S11_to_S15_specialization: S11–S13 → C, S14–S15 → D (both qualify as C or D) ✓
constraint_verification.S3_and_S7_same_OR_different_day: S7=OR2,Day1 | S3=OR2,Day2 → same OR ✓, different days ✓
